web_text: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, to the assembly of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. We always give thanks to God for all of you, mentioning you in our prayers, remembering without ceasing your work of faith and labor of love and perseverance of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, before our God and Father. We know, brothers loved by God, that you are chosen,
slang_text: It's Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy here, writin' to the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. We're always givin' thanks to God for every one of you, mentionin' you in our prayers, never once forgettin' your righteous work built on faith, your labor built on love, and your patience built on hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, right in front of our God and Father. We already know, brothers loved by God, no duh, that you're chosen,

web_text: and that our Good News came to you not in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Spirit, and with much assurance. You know what kind of men we showed ourselves to be among you for your sake. You became imitators of us, and of the Lord, having received the word in much affliction, with joy of the Holy Spirit, so that you became an example to all who believe in Macedonia and in Achaia. For from you the word of the Lord has been declared, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith toward God has gone out; so that we need not to say anything.
slang_text: 'cause our Good News didn't just show up as words to you — it showed up with power, with the Holy Spirit, and with a ton of assurance. You know what kinda guys we turned out to be around you, all for your sake. You started copyin' us, and copyin' the Lord too, takin' in the word through a bunch of hardship, but still with the Holy Spirit's joy, so you turned into a righteous example for every believer out in Macedonia and Achaia. 'Cause the Lord's word got spread from you guys, not just to Macedonia and Achaia but everywhere your faith in God has already reached — so we don't even gotta say a word about it, it speaks for itself.

web_text: For they themselves report concerning us what kind of a reception we had from you; and how you turned to God from idols, to serve a living and true God, and to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead—Jesus, who delivers us from the wrath to come.
slang_text: 'Cause folks are already tellin' everybody what kinda welcome you gave us; how you turned to God, ditchin' those bogus idols, to serve a livin', genuine God, and wait for His Son comin' from heaven — Jesus, who He raised from the dead, the one who's savin' us from the wrath that's still comin'.

web_text: For you yourselves know, brothers, our visit to you wasn’t in vain, but having suffered before and been shamefully treated, as you know, at Philippi, we grew bold in our God to tell you the Good News of God in much conflict. For our exhortation is not of error, nor of uncleanness, nor in deception. But even as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the Good News, so we speak; not as pleasing men, but God, who tests our hearts.
slang_text: 'Cause you guys already know, brothers, our visit to you wasn't for nothin' — after gettin' beat up and shamed, like you already know, back in Philippi, we still got bold in our God, layin' out God's Good News to you right in the middle of a ton of conflict. Our whole pitch here ain't outta some error, or grody motives, or some slick con game. It's the opposite — same as God already checked us out and trusted us with the Good News, that's how we speak; not tryin' to please people, but God, who tests what's really in our hearts.

web_text: For neither were we at any time found using words of flattery, as you know, nor a cloak of covetousness (God is witness), nor seeking glory from men (neither from you nor from others), when we might have claimed authority as apostles of Christ. But we were gentle among you, like a nursing mother cherishes her own children. Even so, affectionately longing for you, we were well pleased to impart to you, not the Good News of God only, but also our own souls, because you had become very dear to us.
slang_text: We never once, like you already know, buttered you up with fake flattery, or hid some greedy angle behind it (God's my witness), or went chasin' glory from people — not from you, not from anybody else — even though we coulda thrown around our authority as Christ's apostles. Instead we were gentle among you, like a mama nursin' and cherishin' her own kids. That's how much we cared about you, achin' to be near you, we were totally stoked to hand you not just God's Good News but our very own souls too, 'cause you'd gotten so dear to us.

web_text: For you remember, brothers, our labor and travail; for working night and day, that we might not burden any of you, we preached to you the Good News of God. You are witnesses with God, how holy, righteously, and blamelessly we behaved ourselves toward you who believe. As you know, we exhorted, comforted, and implored every one of you, as a father does his own children, to the end that you should walk worthily of God, who calls you into his own Kingdom and glory.
slang_text: 'Cause you remember, brothers, our backbreakin' work, grindin' day and night, so we wouldn't burden a single one of you, while we preached you God's Good News. You're witnesses right along with God how holy, righteous, and blameless we acted toward you believers. Like you already know, we encouraged, comforted, and begged every last one of you, like a dad does his own kids, so you'd walk worthy of God, who's callin' you into His own Kingdom and glory.

web_text: For this cause we also thank God without ceasing, that, when you received from us the word of the message of God, you accepted it not as the word of men, but, as it is in truth, the word of God, which also works in you who believe. For you, brothers, became imitators of the assemblies of God which are in Judea in Christ Jesus; for you also suffered the same things from your own countrymen, even as they did from the Jews; who killed both the Lord Jesus and their own prophets, and drove us out, and didn’t please God, and are contrary to all men; forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they may be saved; to fill up their sins always. But wrath has come on them to the uttermost.
slang_text: That's exactly why we never quit thankin' God: when you got God's message from us, you didn't take it as some human's words, you took it as what it really is — God's own word, which is still workin' inside you believers. 'Cause you guys, brothers, ended up copyin' God's churches over in Judea, in Christ Jesus — you suffered the exact same stuff from your own countrymen that they suffered from the Jews, who killed the Lord Jesus and their own prophets, and drove us out, and don't please God, and stand against everybody — way harsh; blockin' us from speakin' to the Gentiles so they could get saved, always pilin' up more sin. But God's wrath has come down on 'em, no doubt, all the way.

web_text: But we, brothers, being bereaved of you for a short season, in presence, not in heart, tried even harder to see your face with great desire, because we wanted to come to you—indeed, I, Paul, once and again—but Satan hindered us. For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Isn’t it even you, before our Lord Jesus at his coming? For you are our glory and our joy.
slang_text: But us, brothers, gettin' torn away from you for a short while — in person, not in heart — we tried even harder to see your faces again, achin' for it, 'cause we wanted to come to you. I'm talkin' me, Paul, more than once — but Satan kept blockin' us. 'Cause what's our hope, or joy, or crown to be stoked about? Isn't it you, standin' right in front of our Lord Jesus when He comes back? You guys are our glory and our joy, plain and simple.

web_text: Therefore when we couldn’t stand it any longer, we thought it good to be left behind at Athens alone, and sent Timothy, our brother and God’s servant in the Good News of Christ, to establish you, and to comfort you concerning your faith; that no one be moved by these afflictions. For you know that we are appointed to this task. For most certainly, when we were with you, we told you beforehand that we are to suffer affliction, even as it happened, and you know.
slang_text: So when we couldn't take it no more, we figured it was better to get left behind alone in Athens, and send Timothy, our brother and God's righteous servant in Christ's Good News, to build you up and comfort you about your faith, so nobody'd get shaken by all this hardship. 'Cause you know we're appointed for this exact deal. Yeah, for real, when we were with you, we already warned you ahead of time that we were gonna suffer hardship, and it happened just like we said, no lie, and you already know that.

web_text: For this cause I also, when I couldn’t stand it any longer, sent that I might know your faith, for fear that by any means the tempter had tempted you, and our labor would have been in vain. But when Timothy came just now to us from you, and brought us glad news of your faith and love, and that you have good memories of us always, longing to see us, even as we also long to see you; for this cause, brothers, we were comforted over you in all our distress and affliction through your faith. For now we live, if you stand fast in the Lord.
slang_text: So that's why I couldn't take it no more either — I sent Timothy to check on your faith, scared that maybe the tempter had gotten to you somehow and all our hard work turned out for nothin', totally bogus. But when Timothy just now got back to us from your place, bringin' good news about your faith and love, tellin' us you still think of us fondly, achin' to see us, same as we're achin' to see you — that's why, brothers, we got comforted over you, right in the middle of all our own distress and hardship, through your faith. 'Cause now we actually feel alive again, for real, long as you're standin' firm in the Lord.

web_text: For what thanksgiving can we render again to God for you, for all the joy with which we rejoice for your sakes before our God; night and day praying exceedingly that we may see your face, and may perfect that which is lacking in your faith? Now may our God and Father himself, and our Lord Jesus Christ, direct our way to you; and the Lord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another, and toward all men, even as we also do toward you,
slang_text: So what kinda thanks could we even give back to God for you, for all the joy we're stoked with over you guys, right in front of our God — prayin' our hearts out, night and day, hopin' to see your faces and finish out whatever's still missin' in your faith? Now may our God and Father Himself, and our Lord Jesus Christ, clear the road for us to reach you; and may the Lord make your love grow and overflow, for each other and for everybody, same as our love overflows for you,

web_text: to the end he may establish your hearts blameless in holiness before our God and Father, at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.
slang_text: so He can lock your hearts in solid, blameless and holy, right in front of our God and Father, when our Lord Jesus shows back up with all His saints.

web_text: Finally then, brothers, we beg and exhort you in the Lord Jesus, that as you received from us how you ought to walk and to please God, that you abound more and more. For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus. For this is the will of God: your sanctification, that you abstain from sexual immorality, that each one of you know how to control his own body in sanctification and honor,
slang_text: So finally, brothers, we're beggin' and pushin' you in the Lord Jesus, that same as you already learned from us how to walk and please God, you'd keep doin' it more and more. You already know what instructions we handed you through the Lord Jesus, no duh. 'Cause this is what God wants: your holiness — that you steer clear of sexual immorality, that every one of you knows how to keep your own body in check, holy and honorable,

web_text: not in the passion of lust, even as the Gentiles who don’t know God; that no one should take advantage of and wrong a brother or sister in this matter; because the Lord is an avenger in all these things, as also we forewarned you and testified. For God called us not for uncleanness, but in sanctification. Therefore he who rejects this doesn’t reject man, but God, who has also given his Holy Spirit to you.
slang_text: not chasin' after cravings and lust like the Gentiles who don't even know God; and that nobody takes advantage of or wrongs a brother or sister over this — 'cause the Lord's gonna square up all that stuff, just like we already warned you and testified. God didn't call us to some grody life, He called us to be holy. So whoever rejects this ain't rejectin' some human — he's rejectin' God, who's already handed you His own Holy Spirit.

web_text: But concerning brotherly love, you have no need that one write to you. For you yourselves are taught by God to love one another, for indeed you do it toward all the brothers who are in all Macedonia. But we exhort you, brothers, that you abound more and more; and that you make it your ambition to lead a quiet life, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, even as we instructed you; that you may walk properly toward those who are outside, and may have need of nothing.
slang_text: But when it comes to lovin' each other like family, you don't even need nobody writin' you about that. God Himself already taught you to love each other, and you're already doin' it toward every single brother across all of Macedonia, for real. But we're pushin' you, brothers, to keep doin' it more and more; and to make it your goal to live a quiet life, mind your own business, work with your own two hands, just like we already told you — so you'd walk right in front of folks outside the faith, and never come up short on nothin'.

web_text: But we don’t want you to be ignorant, brothers, concerning those who have fallen asleep, so that you don’t grieve like the rest, who have no hope. For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep in Jesus. For this we tell you by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive, who are left until the coming of the Lord, will in no way precede those who have fallen asleep. For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with God’s trumpet. The dead in Christ will rise first,
slang_text: But we don't want you clueless, brothers, about the folks who've already passed away, so you don't grieve like the rest of the world, who've got no hope at all — way harsh. 'Cause if we believe Jesus died and rose back up, then God's gonna bring back with Him the ones who died in Jesus too, same deal. Here's what we're tellin' you, straight from the Lord's own word: us who are still alive, still around when the Lord comes back, are not gonna beat the ones who already died to the punch. 'Cause the Lord Himself is gonna come down outta heaven with an epic shout, with the archangel's voice, with God's own trumpet blastin'. The dead in Christ are gonna rise up first,

web_text: then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air. So we will be with the Lord forever. Therefore comfort one another with these words.
slang_text: and then us who are still alive, still hangin' around, are gonna get caught up together with 'em, right up in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the sky. And that's it — we'll be with the Lord forever after that. So comfort each other with these words, no doubt about it.

web_text: But concerning the times and the seasons, brothers, you have no need that anything be written to you. For you yourselves know well that the day of the Lord comes like a thief in the night. For when they are saying, “Peace and safety,” then sudden destruction will come on them, like birth pains on a pregnant woman; and they will in no way escape. But you, brothers, aren’t in darkness, that the day should overtake you like a thief.
slang_text: But when it comes to the times and seasons, brothers, you don't even need us writin' you nothin' about it. You already know good and well the Lord's day shows up like a thief sneakin' in at night. When people are sayin', "Peace and safety," that's exactly when sudden destruction's gonna hit 'em, like labor pains hittin' a pregnant lady — and there's no way they're escapin' it, no how. But you, brothers, you're not stuck in the dark, so that day can't sneak up on you like some thief.

web_text: You are all children of light, and children of the day. We don’t belong to the night, nor to darkness, so then let’s not sleep, as the rest do, but let’s watch and be sober. For those who sleep, sleep in the night; and those who are drunk are drunk in the night. But let us, since we belong to the day, be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love, and, for a helmet, the hope of salvation.
slang_text: You're all kids of the light, kids of the day. We don't belong to the night or the darkness, so let's not go sleepin' like everybody else does — let's stay alert and stay sober instead, no doubt about it. 'Cause folks who sleep, sleep at night, and folks who get wasted, get wasted at night. But us, since we belong to the day, let's stay sober, strappin' on the breastplate of faith and love, and for a helmet, the hope of salvation.

web_text: For God didn’t appoint us to wrath, but to the obtaining of salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ, who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him. Therefore exhort one another, and build each other up, even as you also do. But we beg you, brothers, to know those who labor among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you,
slang_text: 'Cause God didn't set us up for wrath, He set us up to score salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ, who died for us, so whether we're awake or asleep, we'd live together with Him. So push each other on, build each other up, same as you're already doin'. And we're beggin' you, brothers, to respect the folks workin' hard among you, leadin' you in the Lord, correctin' you,

web_text:
and to respect and honor them in love for their work’s sake.
Be at peace among yourselves. We exhort you, brothers, admonish the disorderly, encourage the faint-hearted, support the weak, be patient toward all. See that no one returns evil for evil to anyone, but always follow after that which is good, for one another, and for all. Rejoice always.
slang_text: and to respect and honor 'em with love, all for the sake of the work they're doin'. Stay at peace with each other. We're pushin' you, brothers, call out the troublemakers, encourage the ones losin' heart, back up the weak, stay patient with everybody. Make sure nobody pays back evil with evil — always chase after what's good instead, for each other and for everybody. Be stoked, always.

web_text: Pray without ceasing. In everything give thanks, for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus toward you. Don’t quench the Spirit. Don’t despise prophesies.
slang_text: Pray nonstop, no breaks. Give thanks in everything, 'cause that's what God wants for you in Christ Jesus. Don't snuff out the Spirit. Don't dis prophecies.

web_text: Test all things, and hold firmly that which is good. Abstain from every form of evil. May the God of peace himself sanctify you completely. May your whole spirit, soul, and body be preserved blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ. He who calls you is faithful, who will also do it.
slang_text: Test everything, and hold on tight to whatever's good. Steer clear of every kind of evil, all of it. May the God of peace Himself make you completely holy. May your whole spirit, soul, and body come out blameless when our Lord Jesus Christ shows back up. The One who called you is faithful, and He's gonna pull it off, no doubt.

web_text: Brothers, pray for us. Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss. I solemnly command you by the Lord that this letter be read to all the holy brothers. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
slang_text: Brothers, pray for us. Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss. I'm layin' down a solemn order in the Lord: this letter better get read to every holy brother out there. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
